attachment.content:
Powered by Entire:
  This conversation will be linked to your next commit.

message.content: generate a button for the website that allows the user to export a cleanly formatted pdf of their chat with the agent

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- burnout-check: Helps users recognize and interrupt masking fatigue, burnout, and over-accommodation patterns. Reflects back what the user has described, asks how they are actually doing beyond performance and grades, and treats rest and recovery as legitimate strategies — not last resorts. Does not push users to keep going when they need to stop.
- session-start: Activates at the start of every conversation. Reads the user's opening message, classifies their intent, and routes to the correct skill. Asks exactly one clarifying question if intent is ambiguous before routing. Never gives advice before understanding what the user actually needs.
- situation-decoder: Helps users decode ambiguous academic or social situations: confusing assignment instructions, unclear rubric language, cryptic professor feedback, and socially demanding contexts like presentations, group projects, or advisor meetings. Uses Socratic questions to surface what the user already understands before adding interpretation.
- strategy-toolkit: Recommends a targeted set of strategies for a specific academic or organizational challenge. Always asks about context (environment, energy, time) before recommending anything. Prioritizes non-AI approaches and treats body-doubling, environmental changes, interest-based reframing, and peer strategies as first-class options alongside any tech tools.
- strength-mapper: Translates abstract self-knowledge into one concrete adjusted approach for the specific challenge in front of the user right now. Takes what the user already knows about how they work and applies it to the current situation. Produces one actionable thing, not a general plan.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me explore the project structure to understand the codebase before implementing anything.

input.prompt:
Explore this project at C:\Users\natha\OneDrive\Desktop\school\cos498\CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent. I need to understand:
1. The overall project structure (what kind of app is this?)
2. Where the frontend/UI code lives
3. How the chat interface is built (HTML, React, Vue, etc.)
4. How chat messages are stored/represented in state
5. Any existing CSS files or styling approaches
6. Any existing button components or UI patterns

Please look at the main entry points, key component files, and give me a thorough picture of the chat UI implementation. Be thorough — look at the actual file contents of the key files.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile my findings.

## Comprehensive Project Exploration Report

### 1. **Overall Project Structure & Type**

This is a **Neurodiversity Support Coach** — an AI-powered web application and Claude Code skill-based system designed to help neurodivergent college students (primarily those with ADHD, autism, dyslexia) at the University of Maine turn abstract self-knowledge into concrete, situated action.

The project has two implementations:
- **Web version** (`web_coach.py` + Flask) — deployed on Railway, accessible via browser
- **Claude Code version** (`coach.py` + Claude Agent SDK) — runs in VS Code as an interactive agent using Claude Code skills

**Key Files:**
- `/c/Users/natha/OneDrive/Desktop/school/cos498/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent/web_coach.py` (Flask backend)
- `/c/Users/natha/OneDrive/Desktop/school/cos498/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent/templates/index.html` (Web UI, single-file)
- `/c/Users/natha/OneDrive/Desktop/school/cos498/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent/coach.py` (Claude Agent SDK version)
- `/c/Users/natha/OneDrive/Desktop/school/cos498/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent/requirements.txt` (Flask, gunicorn, anthropic SDK)

---

### 2. **Frontend/UI Code Location**

**All frontend code is in a single file:**
- **`/c/Users/natha/OneDrive/Desktop/school/cos498/CO-4-2-Prototype-class-project-agent/templates/index.html`** (1,132 lines)

There are NO separate React, Vue, or component framework files. This is a vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript application with everything in one file. The backend serves this via Flask's `render_template("index.html")` route at `/`.

---

### 3. **Chat Interface Implementation**

**Type:** Vanilla HTML5 + CSS + plain JavaScript (no frameworks)

**Key architectural patterns:**

**DOM Structure:**
- `.app-shell` — main flex container (100dvh height)
- `.header` — top navigation bar with title, theme toggle, new session button
- `.chat-area` — scrollable message container (flex: 1)
- `.input-area` — message input footer
- `.persona-panel` — slide-out sidebar for user profile (optional)

**Message Rendering:**
Messages are dynamically created with the `appendMessage(role, text)` function (line 1001):
```javascript
function appendMessage(role, text) {
    const row = document.createElement('div');
    row.className = `message-row ${role}`;
    const avatar = document.createElement('div');
    avatar.className = `avatar ${role}`;
    avatar.textContent = role === 'coach' ? '🌱' : 'You';
    const bubble = document.createElement('div');
    bubble.className = `bubble ${role}`;
    bubble.textContent = text;
    row.appendChild(avatar);
    row.appendChild(bubble);
    chatArea.appendChild(row);
    scrollToBottom();
}
```

---

### 4. **Chat Message State Management**

**Conversation storage:**
- **Client-side:** Only `sessionId` and `personaData` are stored in JavaScript variables (lines 850-851, 1053-1054)
- **Server-side:** Flask maintains an in-memory dictionary (`conversations: dict[str, list[dict]]`) that maps session IDs to message histories (line 25 of `web_coach.py`)

**Message format (server-side):**
```python
{"role": "user", "content": "..."} or {"role": "assistant", "content": "..."}
```

**Message flow:**
1. User types in textarea (`#message-input`)
2. `sendMessage()` is called (line 951)
3. Fetch POST to `/chat` endpoint with body: `{message: text, session_id: sessionId, persona: {...}}`
4. Server appends to history, calls Anthropic API (Opus 4.5 fallback to Sonnet 4.5)
5. Response is appended to history, trimmed to last 40 messages (line 254-255)
6. Response returned as JSON, client calls `appendMessage('coach', data.response)`

**State variables:**
- `sessionId` — unique session identifier (generated on first message, line 985)
- `isWaiting` — boolean flag to prevent multiple simultaneous requests (line 851)
- `personaData` — optional user profile object (line 1053)
- `personaSentForSession` — tracks whether persona was already sent for current session (line 1054)

---

### 5. **CSS & Styling Approach**

**Location:** Entire CSS is embedded in `<style>` block in the HTML (lines 10-608)

**Design system (CSS custom properties at `:root`):**

**Typography:**
- Responsive fluid font sizing with clamp() (lines 15-18)
- Font: Inter from Google Fonts, fallback to system-ui/sans-serif

**Spacing scale:**
- `--space-1` through `--space-12` (0.25rem to 3rem)
- Follows a 0.25rem increment pattern

**Border radius tokens:**
- `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, `--radius-xl`, `--radius-full`

**Color palette (Light mode by default, Dark mode at `[data-theme="dark"]`):**

Light mode (lines 32-60):
- **Background:** `--color-bg: #f5f4f0` (warm beige)
- **Surfaces:** `--color-surface: #faf9f6`, `--color-surface-2: #ffffff`
- **Text:** `--color-text: #2a2722` (dark brown), `--color-text-muted: #7a7670`
- **Primary (teal):** `--color-primary: #3d8b7a`
- **User bubble:** `--color-user-bubble: #3d8b7a` (teal, filled)
- **Coach bubble:** `--color-coach-bubble: #ffffff` (white with subtle border)

Dark mode (lines 62-88):
- Background: `#181715`
- Primary: `#5aab99` (lighter teal)
- Adjusted shadows and text colors

**Animation tokens:**
- `--transition: 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` (smooth easing)
- `@keyframes bounce` — typing indicator dots (lines 391-393)
- `@keyframes fadeIn` — message fade-in (lines 395-398)
- `@keyframes slideUp` — message entrance (lines 399-402)

**Responsive breakpoints:**
- Mobile: `@media (max-width: 480px)` (lines 412-419)
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (lines 404-409)

---

### 6. **Button Components & UI Patterns**

**All buttons use semantic HTML `<button>` with consistent class patterns:**

**Primary buttons:**
- `.btn-send` (line 352) — circular icon button, teal background, hover/active states with transform
- `.btn-new-session` (line 168) — pill-shaped, light teal background with border
- `.btn-save-persona` (line 584) — full-width primary action

**Secondary buttons:**
- `.btn-icon` (line 158) — 36x36px circular, transparent, hover shows background
- `.btn-skip-persona` (line 595) — border-only style

**Text input buttons:**
- `.starter-btn` (line 227) — left-aligned text, white background, border, hover shows light primary background

**Interactive states:**
- `:hover` — background/border color changes
- `:active` — darker color + scale(0.95) for click feedback
- `:disabled` — reduced opacity, cursor not-allowed
- `:focus-visible` — 2px primary outline with 3px offset

**Accessibility:**
- All buttons have `aria-label` attributes
- Proper focus-visible outlines
- Theme toggle has dynamic aria-label updates (line 872)
- Message rows have `role="listitem"` and `aria-live="polite"`
- Crisis banner has `role="alert"` with `aria-live="assertive"` (line 652)

---

### 7. **Additional UI Features**

**Welcome screen** (shown until first message):
- Icon, heading, description text
- Four starter button prompts with common opening scenarios (lines 676-692)

**Typing indicator:**
- Shows when waiting for coach response
- Three bouncing dots animation (line 1022-1042)
- Added dynamically with `showTyping()` function

**Crisis detection banner** (lines 651-655):
- Hidden by default, shows when keywords detected
- Contains 988 and UMaine counseling resources
- Toggles with `.visible` class based on crisis keywords (lines 940-948)

**Persona/Profile panel** (lines 721-841):
- Fixed right-side slide-in sidebar (line 468-481)
- Optional fields: name, age, conditions (checkboxes), backstory, triggers, strengths, success goals
- `.persona-tab` trigger on right edge (vertical text)
- Overlay click closes panel
- Escape key also closes

**Theme toggle:**
- Dynamic SVG icon swap (sun/moon) based on current theme (line 877-889)
- Uses `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` to detect system preference initially

**Input textarea:**
- Auto-resizing textarea (max 160px height) (lines 892-895, 336-349)
- Shift+Enter for new line, Enter to send (lines 898-903)
- Placeholder: "What's on your mind?"
- Spell-check enabled, autocomplete off

---

### 8. **Backend Integration (web_coach.py)**

**Framework:** Flask 3.0+

**Key routes:**
- `GET /` — serves the HTML template
- `POST /chat` — handles message, manages session history, calls Anthropic API
- `POST /new-session` — clears session history
- `POST /set-persona` — accepts persona data (for potential future use)
- `GET /healthz` — health check for deployment

**System prompt construction (lines 53-146):**
- Loads all five SKILL.md files at startup
- Injects them into a detailed system prompt with hard rules (one question per response, progress through skill arcs, etc.)
- Claude API receives full skill definitions as context, not as tool calls

**API fallback strategy (lines 231-246):**
- Tries Claude Opus 4.5 first
- Falls back to Claude Sonnet 4.5 if API error occurs

**Session management:**
- Each session gets unique UUID (line 158)
- Conversations stored in memory with 40-message trimming (lines 253-255)
- Persona injected as a system message on first real message of session (lines 169-200)

---

### 9. **Data Flow Example**

1. User visits `/` → HTML loaded
2. User types "I have a paper due..." → calls `sendMessage()`
3. Fetch POST to `/chat` with `{message, session_id: null, persona: {name, conditions, etc.}}`
4. Flask creates new session ID, appends persona context to history
5. Claude receives full skill definitions + persona + formatted message
6. Response generated by Claude
7. JSON returned: `{response: "...", session_id: "uuid-123"}`
8. Client stores `sessionId = "uuid-123"` and calls `appendMessage('coach', response)`
9. On next message, `session_id` is included, history retrieved and extended
10. Typing indicator shown during fetch, removed when response arrives

---

### 10. **Key Technical Characteristics**

**Frontend approach:**
- Single-page application (SPA-like behavior but not a framework-based SPA)
- No build process, no transpilation, pure vanilla JS
- Responsive design with CSS custom properties
- Smooth animations and transitions

**Backend approach:**
- Stateless API except for in-memory session store (lost on restart)
- Full skill definitions embedded in system prompt (not using tools)
- Single Claude API client with model fallback
- No database, no persistence layer

**Accessibility:**
- ARIA labels and roles throughout
- Focus management (focus input on load, focus close button when panel opens)
- Semantic HTML
- Keyboard navigation (Escape to close panel, Enter to send)

**Mobile responsiveness:**
- Max-width 760px for chat (line 113)
- Responsive padding/font sizes at <480px breakpoint
- 100dvh for proper viewport behavior on mobile

---

## Summary

This is a **thoughtfully designed, accessible chat interface** built with:
- **Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS** (no frameworks)
- **Flask backend** with in-memory session management
- **Anthropic Claude API** as the brain
- **Skill-based architecture** (five skills for routing and responding)
- **Warm, UX-focused design** using a sage/teal color palette
- **Full accessibility** with ARIA, semantic HTML, focus management
- **Optional persona panel** for personalization without requiring account signup

All UI code lives in one HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript. The styling uses modern CSS custom properties and responsive techniques. Chat messages are stored server-side in a simple list structure and managed by session ID.

- No build process, no transpilation, pure vanilla JS
- Responsive design with CSS custom properties
- Smooth animations and transitions

**Backend approach:**
- Stateless API except for in-memory session store (lost on restart)
- Full skill definitions embedded in system prompt (not using tools)
- Single Claude API client with model fallback
- No database, no persistence layer

**Accessibility:**
- ARIA labels and roles throughout
- Focus management (focus input on load, focus close button when panel opens)
- Semantic HTML
- Keyboard navigation (Escape to close panel, Enter to send)

**Mobile responsiveness:**
- Max-width 760px for chat (line 113)
- Responsive padding/font sizes at <480px breakpoint
- 100dvh for proper viewport behavior on mobile

---

## Summary

This is a **thoughtfully designed, accessible chat interface** built with:
- **Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS** (no frameworks)
- **Flask backend** with in-memory session management
- **Anthropic Claude API** as the brain
- **Skill-based architecture** (five skills for routing and responding)
- **Warm, UX-focused design** using a sage/teal color palette
- **Full accessibility** with ARIA, semantic HTML, focus management
- **Optional persona panel** for personalization without requiring account signup

All UI code lives in one HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript. The styling uses modern CSS custom properties and responsive techniques. Chat messages are stored server-side in a simple list structure and managed by session ID.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" data-theme="light">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Neurodiversity Support Coach</title>
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300..600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />
  <style>
    /* ── Design tokens ─────────────────────────────────────── */
    :root {
      --font-body: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;

      --text-xs:   clamp(0.75rem,  0.7rem  + 0.25vw, 0.875rem);
      --text-sm:   clamp(0.875rem, 0.8rem  + 0.35vw, 1rem);
      --text-base: clamp(1rem,     0.95rem + 0.25vw, 1.125rem);
      --text-lg:   clamp(1.125rem, 1rem    + 0.75vw, 1.5rem);

      --space-1: 0.25rem; --space-2: 0.5rem;  --space-3: 0.75rem;
      --space-4: 1rem;    --space-5: 1.25rem; --space-6: 1.5rem;
      --space-8: 2rem;    --space-10: 2.5rem; --space-12: 3rem;

      --radius-sm: 0.375rem;
      --radius-md: 0.5rem;
      --radius-lg: 0.75rem;
      --radius-xl: 1rem;
      --radius-full: 9999px;

      --transition: 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);

      /* Light mode — warm sage palette */
      --color-bg:             #f5f4f0;
      --color-surface:        #faf9f6;
      --color-surface-2:      #ffffff;
      --color-border:         #dedad4;
      --color-divider:        #e8e5e0;

      --color-text:           #2a2722;
      --color-text-muted:     #7a7670;
      --color-text-faint:     #b5b2ad;
      --color-text-inverse:   #f9f8f4;

      --color-primary:        #3d8b7a;
      --color-primary-hover:  #2e7063;
      --color-primary-active: #1f5549;
      --color-primary-light:  #e6f2f0;
      --color-primary-mid:    #c2ddd9;

      --color-user-bubble:    #3d8b7a;
      --color-user-text:      #ffffff;
      --color-coach-bubble:   #ffffff;
      --color-coach-border:   #e0dbd5;

      --color-warning:        #8b5a2b;

      --shadow-sm: 0 1px 3px rgba(40, 35, 25, 0.08);
      --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(40, 35, 25, 0.10);
      --shadow-lg: 0 8px 24px rgba(40, 35, 25, 0.12);
    }

    [data-theme="dark"] {
      --color-bg:             #181715;
      --color-surface:        #1e1d1b;
      --color-surface-2:      #252422;
      --color-border:         #363330;
      --color-divider:        #2c2a28;

      --color-text:           #d4d1cc;
      --color-text-muted:     #7a7875;
      --color-text-faint:     #555350;
      --color-text-inverse:   #2b2a28;

      --color-primary:        #5aab99;
      --color-primary-hover:  #3d9080;
      --color-primary-active: #2a7367;
      --color-primary-light:  #1e2e2c;
      --color-primary-mid:    #2a403d;

      --color-user-bubble:    #3d8b7a;
      --color-user-text:      #ffffff;
      --color-coach-bubble:   #252422;
      --color-coach-border:   #363330;

      --shadow-sm: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
      --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.35);
      --shadow-lg: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.45);
    }

    /* ── Reset ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
    html { -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; scroll-behavior: smooth; }
    body {
      font-family: var(--font-body);
      font-size: var(--text-base);
      color: var(--color-text);
      background: var(--color-bg);
      min-height: 100dvh;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;

content.text: Let me do a quick sanity check on the key sections to make sure everything looks right.

All six touchpoints are in place. The implementation is complete:

- **CSS** (line 181): `.btn-export` styled to match the existing `New session` button — same teal palette, pill shape, hover/active states
- **HTML button** (line 655): placed between "New session" and the theme toggle in the header, with a download arrow SVG icon
- **jsPDF CDN** (line 874): loaded synchronously before the inline script so it's ready on click
- **DOM ref** (line 889): `btnExport` wired up
- **Event listener** (line 937): calls `exportToPDF` on click
- **`exportToPDF` function** (line 1082): builds a letter-format PDF with:
  - Teal header with app name + University of Maine + today's date
  - Horizontal rule separator
  - Each message labeled **YOU** or **COACH** in matching teal tones, body text below
  - Thin dividers between messages
  - Automatic page breaks for long conversations
  - Page-number footer (`1 / N`) on every page
  - Saves as `coach-session-YYYY-MM-DD.pdf`
